headline: Intel results to show if supply chain issues are dimming its AI ambitions
summary: Intel investors on Thursday will focus on its efforts to sort out ‌supply chain issues that have limited the company's ‌ability to ramp up chip production to meet rising demand from businesses ​adopting AI-related services.  Intel has warned that supply constraints for its server chips that are used alongside graphics processors made by companies such as Nvidia will be most acute in ‌the first quarter before ⁠easing in the second.  • Intel is expected to report a 1.9% decline in first-quarter revenue ⁠to $12.42 billion and a near 90% drop in adjusted earnings per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

raw.headline: Intel results to show if supply chain issues are dimming its AI ambitions
raw.summary: Intel investors on Thursday will focus on its efforts to sort out ‌supply chain issues that have limited the company's ‌ability to ramp up chip production to meet rising demand from businesses ​adopting AI-related services.  Intel has warned that supply constraints for its server chips that are used alongside graphics processors made by companies such as Nvidia will be most acute in ‌the first quarter before ⁠easing in the second.  • Intel is expected to report a 1.9% decline in first-quarter revenue ⁠to $12.42 billion and a near 90% drop in adjusted earnings per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

headline: Australia's Syenta raises $26 million to ease AI chip bottleneck, former Intel CEO joins board
summary: Syenta, an Australian semiconductor technology startup, said on Tuesday that it has raised $26 million for a new manufacturing technique ‌that could help ease persistent supply chain bottlenecks for artificial intelligence.  Along with the ‌funding, the company said it will open an office in the U.S. state of Arizona, near the manufacturing facilities ​of Intel and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, and that former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger will be joining its board of directors.  Modern AI chips such as those from Nvidia, Google and others are not a single chip, but rather multiple chips bonded together with what is known as ‌advanced packaging technology, mostly from TSMC.

raw.headline: Australia's Syenta raises $26 million to ease AI chip bottleneck, former Intel CEO joins board

Modern AI chips such as those from Nvidia, Google and others are not a single chip, but rather multiple chips bonded together with what is known as ‌advanced packaging technology, mostly from TSMC.

headline: Google Broadcom Chip Deal Puts Intel AI Foundry Goals Under Scrutiny
summary: Google is expanding its custom chip partnership with Broadcom, testing Intel's efforts to grow its AI focused foundry and custom silicon business. The shift affects one of Intel's key hyperscale relationships at a time when the company is highlighting progress in AI manufacturing and large projects such as Terafab. Investors are watching how this development fits into Intel's long term foundry roadmap and its position in advanced custom AI chips. Intel (NasdaqGS:INTC) enters this news cycle...
